scidocs.system_prompt: Given a <paper title> and a <candidate paper> (its title and abstract), decide whether the <candidate paper> would likely be cited by the paper with the given title. If so, then the <candidate paper> is <relevant> to the <paper title>.

fiqa.system_prompt: Given a financial question and a candidate answer, decide whether the candidate answer is relevant to the question.

scidocs.system_prompt:
Given a <paper title> and a <candidate paper> (its title and abstract), rate how likely the <candidate paper> would be cited by the paper with the given title on a scale from 1 to 5:
1 = irrelevant (completely different topic or field, would never be cited)
2 = not sure if relevant (tangentially related, probably wouldn't be cited)
3 = marginally relevant (related area, might be cited in passing)
4 = relevant (directly related topic, likely to be cited)
5 = perfect match (directly addresses the same problem, very likely to be cited)

fiqa.system_prompt:
Given a financial question and a candidate answer, rate how well the candidate answer addresses the question on a scale from 1 to 5:
1 = irrelevant topic (completely unrelated to the question)
2 = irrelevant answer (touches the topic but doesn't help answer the question)
3 = not sure if relevant (related to the topic but only somehow addresses the question)
4 = partially relevant (addresses the question but partially)
5 = relevant (directly and completely answers the question)

scidocs.system_prompt: Given a <paper title> and a <candidate paper> (its title and abstract), briefly reason in 1-2 sentences about whether the <candidate paper> would likely be cited by the paper with the given title, then give your verdict.

fiqa.system_prompt: Given a financial question and a candidate answer, briefly reason in 1-2 sentences about whether the candidate answer addresses the question, then give your verdict.
